question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `nodes.gossipFqdnRestricted` is hardcoded to `'false'` by submitting crafted contract calls that cause the EVM executor to route gossip messages to attacker-controlled FQDN endpoints, effectively preventing legitimate transaction gossip from reaching consensus nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the `props.putAll(properties)` override mechanism — where user-defined `hiero.mirror.web3.evm.properties` entries overwrite all defaults — to inject a `nodes.gossipFqdnRestricted=true` override that silently blocks gossip of all subsequent transactions processed by the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user, by exploiting the `props.putAll(properties)` override at line 172, inject a crafted `executor.disableThrottles=false` override that re-enables throttles and causes the EVM executor to drop or queue transactions before they are gossiped, effectively preventing their propagation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `contracts.throttle.throttleByGas` is set to `'false'` and `executor.disableThrottles` is set to `'true'` to flood the EVM executor with maximum-gas transactions (up to `maxGasLimit` of 15,000,000), exhausting processing capacity and preventing legitimate transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the disabled `contracts.throttle.throttleByOpsDuration=false` setting to submit a burst of opcode-heavy contract calls that saturate the EVM executor thread pool, causing a backlog that prevents new transactions from being picked up and gossiped to the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user leverage the `jumboTransactions.allowedHederaFunctionalities` property (which permits `ContractCall,ContractCreate,EthereumTransaction`) to craft oversized jumbo transactions that overwhelm the gossip layer's message size limits, causing gossip of those transactions — and potentially adjacent ones — to be silently dropped?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `validatePayerBalance=false` default to submit unlimited zero-balance contract calls that are accepted and queued for gossip, flooding the gossip queue and causing legitimate transactions to be evicted or delayed?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `fees.simpleFeesEnabled=false` setting — which disables simple fee enforcement — to submit transactions with zero or negligible fees that bypass cost barriers, enabling mass transaction submission that prevents gossip of other transactions through queue saturation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `contracts.systemContract.scheduleService.scheduleCall.enabled=true` setting to schedule a large number of future contract calls that, when triggered simultaneously, overwhelm the gossip mechanism and prevent other transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `nodes.minNodeRewardBalance=Long.MAX_VALUE` setting — which is deliberately set to fail reward conditions — to trigger repeated reward-check failures in the upstream consensus library that cause transaction processing to stall, preventing gossip of pending transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the combination of `nodes.nodeRewardsEnabled=true` and `nodes.preserveMinNodeRewardBalance=true` with `nodes.minNodeRewardBalance=Long.MAX_VALUE` to trigger an infinite reward-balance-check loop in the upstream library, causing the EVM executor to hang and preventing gossip of any transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `tss.hintsEnabled=false` and `tss.historyEnabled=false` settings — which disable TSS hint and history tracking — to craft transactions that exploit TSS-dependent code paths in the upstream library, causing exceptions that abort gossip of those transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject `tss.hintsEnabled=true` or `tss.historyEnabled=true`, enabling TSS code paths that the mirror node is not designed to support, causing the EVM executor to crash or deadlock and preventing gossip of all subsequent transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject a `contracts.chainId` value that mismatches the actual network chain ID, causing the EVM executor to reject all transactions as invalid chain and preventing their gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject a `ledger.id` value that does not match the actual network ledger ID, causing the upstream consensus library to reject all transactions as belonging to a different ledger and preventing their gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject a `contracts.evm.version` value pointing to a non-existent or incompatible EVM version, causing the EVM executor to fail initialization and preventing gossip of all transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject `contracts.maxRefundPercentOfGasLimit=0`, causing all gas refund calculations to return zero and triggering downstream assertion failures in the upstream library that abort transaction processing and prevent gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject `jumboTransactions.allowedHederaFunctionalities` with an empty or invalid value, causing the upstream library to reject all `ContractCall`, `ContractCreate`, and `EthereumTransaction` types and preventing their gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject `contracts.systemContract.scheduleService.scheduleCall.enabled=false`, disabling the schedule service and causing all scheduled transactions to fail silently, preventing their gossip to the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject `hedera.realm` or `hedera.shard` values that differ from the actual network values, causing the upstream library to route or reject transactions for the wrong realm/shard and preventing their gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject `nodes.gossipFqdnRestricted=true` combined with a crafted FQDN allowlist that excludes all real consensus nodes, effectively preventing any transaction from being gossiped to the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject `nodes.nodeRewardsEnabled=false`, disabling node rewards and causing the upstream library to skip reward-related transaction processing steps, potentially causing those transactions to be dropped before gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject `contracts.sidecars` with a large or malformed value, causing the upstream library to attempt to process invalid sidecar data and throwing exceptions that abort transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getSemanticEvmVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the `ContractCallContext` block parameter to trigger `useHistorical()=true` with a crafted block index that maps to an extremely old EVM version (e.g., `EVM_VERSION_0_30`), causing the EVM executor to use a version that lacks support for current transaction types and preventing their gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getSemanticEvmVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a historical block number that causes `getEvmVersionForBlock()` to return a mismatched EVM version, leading the upstream consensus library to reject the transaction as incompatible and preventing its gossip?
